- Row 18: ccccBBBBccccBBBBccccBBBB (charcoal, black, charcoal, black, charcoal, black)
- Row 22: BBBBYYYYBBBBMMMMBBBBcccc (black, yellow, black, magenta, black, charcoal)

This suggests that the right buttons (which I turned OFF) were controlling the visibility of the colored objects vs. the checkerboard pattern. When right buttons are OFF, the checkerboard is fully visible. When right buttons are ON, the colored objects are visible.

Actually, let me reconsider. The right panel changed because I clicked the preview box, which toggled the left H buttons. Maybe the left buttons control the right panel, not the left panel?
Or maybe the left and right buttons are interconnected in some way.

- Left H buttons: control something related to horizontal movement or visibility
- Left V buttons: control something related to vertical movement or visibility
- Right H buttons: control something related to horizontal movement or visibility
- Right V buttons: control something related to vertical movement or visibility
- Preview boxes: toggle left button states and select movement direction
- Trigger: moves the T-shape based on selected preview box
